source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager / AI-Native Product Development
source_jobs.description:
Opportunity

Our Product team is growing, and we're looking for a Senior Product Manager / AI-Native Product Development to lead one of Method's most important product surfaces: Connect & Integrations. This role will start with deep ownership of our QuickBooks Online sync, APIs, and related integration experiences. Over time, the opportunity is broader: helping Method evolve the systems that connect our CRM to the business-critical tools our customers rely on every day, while expanding our platform to support a more connected, automated, and AI-enabled future. This is a high-impact role for a senior individual contributor who combines strong product fundamentals with a builder orientation — someone who uses AI, prototypes, automations, technical artifacts, and hands-on experimentation to move from idea to customer value faster. We are looking for someone who can clearly articulate how technical investments improve customer trust, activation, retention, support efficiency, and long-term product value. The right person will own outcomes, work closely with engineering, use AI natively, and help model Method's emerging Product Architect function — a hybrid product role that combines customer judgment, systems thinking, technical curiosity, and the ability to work closer to the product surface.


About Method

Method is on a mission to transform how small businesses operate by giving them real control over their customer workflows. Our flagship product, Method CRM is purpose-built for small businesses that have outgrown spreadsheets and generic tools. With deep QuickBooks integration and a powerful no-code customization engine, Method enables users to design workflows that truly fit their unique needs. As a scaling growth company, we're now taking that mission further, leveraging AI to develop intelligent agents that assist users in rapidly building and customizing no-code apps. This innovation is at the heart of our next chapter, and we're looking for people who are excited to build the future of work!

At Method, doing fantastic work is just as important as looking out for each other and our community. From flex hours, benefits and monthly team events to our Method:Gives program, we do our best to support both our employees and community.


The Role

As Senior Product Manager / AI-Native Product Development, Connect & Integrations, you will own the product strategy, roadmap, and outcomes for the systems that connect Method with QuickBooks, APIs, and other business-critical platforms. Your immediate focus will be on our data sync customer experiences that include setup, reliability, visibility, troubleshooting, and trust. You will also help define the broader future of Method's integration strategy, including APIs, ecosystem expansion, partner integrations, and connected workflow opportunities. This is not a traditional roadmap-management PM role. The right person understands that integrations are not just back-end plumbing. They are core to customer trust, workflow continuity, activation, retention, and Method's ability to grow as a connected platform. You will work closely with engineering, design, support, customer success, sales, and product leadership to turn complex technical work into clear customer and business outcomes. You will also help define and model Method's emerging Product Architect function by setting an example for high-agency, technically curious, builder-oriented product work.


What You'll Do

Product Strategy & Roadmap:

 - Own the product strategy and roadmap for Method's Connect & Integrations surface.

 - Lead product work across QuickBooks Online sync, APIs, integration workflows, and related customer experiences.

 - Balance near-term customer needs with long-term platform, reliability, and ecosystem investments.

 - Identify opportunities to expand Method's integration ecosystem beyond today's core accounting sync use cases.

 - Translate company priorities into clear product bets, sequencing, and mea

source_jobs.title: Senior Full-Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
OPPORTUNITY

Our Development team is growing, and we’re searching for a Senior Full-Stack Developer to join us. We need someone who loves building solid, high-quality features and is excited to work with modern technologies. This is a hands-on coding role, but you’ll also have plenty of opportunities to step up, lead projects, and mentor your teammates. We’re on a mission to build the future of our platform with AI-powered tools that help users build and customize their no-code apps. If you’re a key player in this kind of innovation, we want to talk to you.



About Method

Method is on a mission to transform how small businesses operate by giving them real control over their customer workflows. Our flagship product, Method CRM is purpose-built for small businesses that have outgrown spreadsheets and generic tools. With deep QuickBooks integration and a powerful no-code customization engine, Method enables users to design workflows that truly fit their unique needs. As a scaling growth company, we’re now taking that mission further, leveraging AI to develop intelligent agents that assist users in rapidly building and customizing no-code apps. This innovation is at the heart of our next chapter, and we’re looking for people who are excited to build the future of work!


The Role

You’ll join our No-Code/AI team and own significant features from design all the way through to production.

 - AI product development. You’ll build the systems behind two core products: an app builder where users describe what they want and the system generates it, and a conversational workflow engine where users interact with an AI agent to move work through their business. That means working with LLMs, vector stores, embeddings, and orchestration frameworks to create AI-driven features that are reliable and usable by non-technical small business owners. The real challenge isn’t wiring up API calls — it’s designing the right abstractions so AI capabilities integrate cleanly into a no-code platform where users need predictable, composable behavior.

 - Full-stack ownership. Our backend is C#/.NET with SQL Server and MongoDB; frontend is React/TypeScript. You’ll work across both, making decisions about data modeling, API contracts, state management, and event-driven flows with RabbitMQ/SQS. These are your decisions to make — and yours to stand behind.

 - Quality and standards. You’ll set the bar for testing, code quality, and architectural discipline. Your code reviews will make the team sharper. You’ll mentor junior and intermediate developers — not through lectures, but through how you build and how you explain your thinking.

 - Operations. You’ll ship to AWS infrastructure (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD) and own what happens after deployment — monitoring, observability, and understanding how your systems behave under real load.



How We Work

We’re an AI-first engineering team. We use AI tools across coding, review, testing, and documentation, and we expect everyone on the team to do the same. Our approach to AI tooling is permissive — we default to allowing new tools rather than restricting them. If something makes you more effective, use it.



The Team

You’ll be joining a team of nine: an architect, four senior developers (including yourself), one intermediate developer, and two QAs. It’s a small, experienced group where everyone has real ownership over their work and direct influence on the product. You’ll report to a Development Manager.



What You Bring

Before you read this next section, we want you to know that we know the “ideal candidate” doesn’t exist — everybody has a unique background and skill set. Even if you don’t check every box below, we’d love for you to apply – we are building for the future and growth potential is a priority for us!

 - 7+ years building production web applications, with real depth in both backend (C#/.NET or a comparable strongly-typed ecosystem) and frontend (React/TypeScript)

 - Strong system design thinkin

source_jobs.title: Junior Solutions Specialist
source_jobs.description:
OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a results-oriented individual who wants to take their career in customer experience to the next level. We believe that enabling a customer for success doesn’t end at implementation. We understand that some customers require ongoing support to be fully enabled for long-term success, and that is where you come in!

The successful candidate we are looking for will be responsible for implementing, customizing and integrating Method CRM into our customers’ businesses. This person will also provide paid training sessions and work as a dedicated consultant to our customers.




ABOUT METHOD

Method is on a mission to transform how small businesses operate by giving them real control over their customer workflows. Our flagship product, Method CRM is purpose-built for small businesses that have outgrown spreadsheets and generic tools. With deep QuickBooks integration and a powerful no-code customization engine, Method enables users to design workflows that truly fit their unique needs. As a scaling growth company, we’re now taking that mission further, leveraging AI to develop intelligent agents that assist users in rapidly building and customizing no-code apps. This innovation is at the heart of our next chapter, and we’re looking for people who are excited to build the future of work!




THE ROLE

As a Junior Solutions Specialist, you will be part of a driven and supportive team that’s always striving to provide the highest level of customer service, while also utilizing Method’s platform to create customized solutions for our customer’s needs.

 - Gather and document business requirements to better understand a customer’s business and scope of work.

 - Translate business requirements into designs, data models and technical requirements.

 - Scope, design, develop, test and refine customized Method CRM solutions.

 - Monitor and manage a project’s hour allocations, scope, and schedule to ensure they are delivered on-time and on-budget.

 - Be a trusted advisor to the customer, understanding the customer’s end-to-end business processes and areas for future growth.

 - Provide consistent feedback to the entire team on enhancements we can make to product development.




WHAT YOU BRING

Before you read this next section, we want you to know that we know the “ideal candidate” doesn’t exist — everybody has a unique background and skill set. Even if you don’t check every box below, we’d love for you to apply if you’re passionate about creating custom solutions and the opportunity at Method. We are building for the future and growth potential is a priority for us!

 - Post-secondary education in the following areas preferred: Business Technology Management, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Software Engineering or equivalent training/experience.

 - Experience in CRM and SQL is preferred.

 - Excellent organizational, presentation and communication skills with an engaging phone presence.

 - Ability to meet tight deadlines.

 - Must have a strong technical aptitude and ability to learn new software quickly.

 - Strong problem solving and critical thinking skills. You must be passionate about your ability to always find solutions to customers’ needs.

 - Must be self-motivated, goal drive and possess the ability to multitask and prioritize

 - Must have an entrepreneurial spirit, strong ethics and integrity along with the desire to exceed every expectation.

 - A team player with a positive attitude and a willingness to contribute to the overall team’s success in a fast paced environment.

 - Bonus points if you are fluent in French or Spanish.




WHAT WE OFFER

🌐 Flexible hybrid work model – designed to support work-life balance while maintaining strong team collaboration; employees work from our downtown Toronto office 2–3 days per week

🎉 Collaborative and social team culture – work in a supportive environment with regular team events and company-wide celebrations that keep things fun and connected

source_jobs.title: Intermediate Full-Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
Opportunity

Our Development team is growing, and we're looking for a .NET Full-Stack Developer who's excited to build high-quality, customer-facing features and solve real business problems. You'll work across our stack - C#/.NET, React, and our in-house Storybook component library - on a platform backed by AWS, serverless (Lambda), and a messaging-based architecture. We're AI-first but human-accountable: you'll use AI to move fast, and your judgment to make sure what ship is right.


Admin Team:
The Admin team owns the core administrative and security capabilities of Method CRM, including authentication, user management, access control, account configuration and multi-tenancy. We are a balanced full-stack team building both frontend and backend experiences that enable customers to securely manage their organizations at scale. Our work underpins platform trust, compliance, and long-term customer growth.


About Method

Method is on a mission to transform how small businesses operate by giving them real control over their customer workflows. Our flagship product, Method CRM is purpose-built for small businesses that have outgrown spreadsheets and generic tools. With deep QuickBooks integration and a powerful no-code customization engine, Method enables users to design workflows that truly fit their unique needs. As a scaling growth company, we’re now taking that mission further, leveraging AI to develop intelligent agents that assist users in rapidly building and customizing no-code apps. This innovation is at the heart of our next chapter, and we’re looking for people who are excited to build the future of work!


The Role

As a .NET Full-Stack Developer, you will be part of a driven and supportive team that’s always striving to deliver value to our customers by shipping new features and helping our platform scale.

 - Design, develop, maintain, and deploy well-tested services and features, with a focus on solving the underlying customer problems.

 - Refine project requirements prior to development, ensuring that ambiguity is resolved early and that delivery remains within the appropriate scope.

 - Provide in-depth analysis and resolutions to complex problems reported by our users, targeting the root cause of every issue.

 - Effectively contribute suggestions and new ideas to enhance the performance and usability of our platform for all users.

 - Participate in team story and task breakdowns, code reviews, and retrospectives to drive continuous improvement.

 - Communicate early with leadership when technical work impacts core architecture, security, or deviates from established objectives.



What You Bring

Before you read this next section, we want you to know that we know the “ideal candidate” doesn’t exist — everybody has a unique background and skill set. Even if you don’t check every box below, we’d love for you to apply - we are building for the future and growth potential is a priority for us!

 - 3+ years building web apps across both front-end and back-end.

 - Strong C#, .NET Core, .NET 6/7/8, Web API, MVC, REST, design patterns, SPA, monolith vs. microservices, and Git.

 - JavaScript, HTML5, CSS, and a front-end SPA library (Angular / React).

 - Persistence storage and ORMs (SQL / NoSQL).

 - Unit and integration testing; Agile (Sprint / Kanban) experience.

 - Works AI-first with disciplined verification — uses AI to accelerate analysis, code, and tests; neither avoids it nor trusts it blindly.

 - Self-motivated and goal-driven, prioritizes well, and enjoys problem solving in a fast-paced, collaborative team.

 - Passionate for building engaging user experiences.

 - A team player with a positive attitude and a willingness to contribute to the overall team’s success in a fast paced environment.

 - Education in Computer Science, Computer/Software Engineering, or equivalent experience preferred


Bonus points if you have any of the following:

 - Infra and front-end tooling: Linux, Docker, Redux, MongoDB,

source_jobs.title: Intermediate Full-Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
Opportunity

Method is the operating system thousands of small businesses run their day on — purpose-built for QuickBooks and Xero users who have outgrown spreadsheets and generic tools. Our no-code customization engine lets customers design workflows that fit their business, and we’re now layering AI on top to help them build and automate those workflows faster than ever.



The Growth team owns the moment that matters most: turning a curious signup into a committed subscriber. We work on the top-of-funnel journey — signup, onboarding, activation, and billing — running continuous experiments to improve conversion at every step. Everything we do is in service of one goal: increasing the percentage of signups that subscribe.



We’re looking for an Intermediate Full-Stack Developer who wants to ship work that moves real metrics for real businesses, and who already works AI-first in their craft.



The Role

You’ll join a focused, cross-functional Growth team. Your work lives in the top-of-funnel stack: signup UI, account API, analytics, billing, onboarding, and subscription — systems that directly affect whether a new user stays or leaves.



As an Intermediate Full-Stack Developer, you’ll operate with periodic review and coaching from your manager and team lead, own implementation decisions within approved experiment scope, and grow toward stronger autonomy over time.



 - Ship experiment variations end-to-end across React / TypeScript and .NET Core — from hypothesis to production.

 - Work across the full user journey — the systems that bring new users in, get them to value, and keep them subscribed.

 - Contribute to improving the early customer experience, helping users find value in the product as quickly as possible.

 - Support a data-driven team by helping instrument and monitor the features you build.

 - Operate in an AI-native planning → execution → verification loop: use AI to understand requirements, generate and refactor code, and validate before shipping. Own what you ship.

 - Write automated tests alongside your implementations, working closely with QA to meet quality standards.

 - Collaborate closely with your team across planning, delivery, and regular cross-functional touchpoints.

 - Escalate early when experiment scope expands, architecture is affected, customer data integrity is at risk, or AI-generated output can’t be safely verified.



What You Bring

We know the ideal candidate doesn’t exist. If you don’t check every box, we’d still love to hear from you — growth potential is real here. That said, the AI-first bar, trust-and-verify judgment, and customer-trust instinct are non-negotiable regardless of background.



 - 3+ years building and shipping production web applications across front-end and back-end.

 - Strong backend fundamentals in C# / .NET Core, or equivalent in Java, Node, or a comparable stack.

 - Strong frontend fundamentals in React / TypeScript, or equivalent in Angular or Vue.

 - Experience with relational and / or NoSQL persistence and ORM.

 - Solid grasp of API design, real-world debugging, and writing clean, maintainable code.

 - Hands-on experience with unit and integration testing as a first-class part of delivery.

 - Comfortable with experiment-driven development: shipping lean MVPs, reading results, and iterating.

 - Comfortable with tasks with clear direction and close coaching; communicates trade-offs and uncertainty clearly.

 - AI-first in your daily workflow (Codex, Copilot, Claude, or equivalent) with the engineering judgment to understand and own what you ship.

 - Sprint / Kanban or ScrumBan experience in an Agile environment



Bonus points if you have any of the following:

 - Experience with product analytics tools — Amplitude, Datadog, or similar.

 - Familiarity with A / B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, or experimentation frameworks.

 - Experience with billing or subscription systems.

 - Experience with no-code or low-code platforms, or produc

source_jobs.title: Intermediate Quality Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Opportunity

Our Development team is growing and we’re looking for a Quality Engineer who is passionate about mastering the best practices of Test Engineering, employing various techniques such as Unit Testing, Integration Testing, End-to-End Testing (browser based), and helping us in implementing a modern Continuous Delivery pipeline. We’re AI-first but human-accountable — you’ll use AI to move faster, and your judgment to make sure what we ship is right. Your work will have tremendous impact, allowing your team to rapidly release code to customers with confidence.



About Method

Method is on a mission to transform how small businesses operate by giving them real control over their customer workflows. Our flagship product, Method CRM is purpose-built for small businesses that have outgrown spreadsheets and generic tools. With deep QuickBooks integration and a powerful no-code customization engine, Method enables users to design workflows that truly fit their unique needs. As a scaling growth company, we’re now taking that mission further, leveraging AI to develop intelligent agents that assist users in rapidly building and customizing no-code apps. This innovation is at the heart of our next chapter, and we’re looking for people who are excited to build the future of work!



The Role

As a Quality Engineer, you will be part of a driven and supportive team that’s always striving to deliver value to our customers by:

 - Developing high quality Integration Tests, End-to-End Tests and Performance Tests to ensure fast and reliable iterative development.

 - Create test plans and test cases in an Agile environment.

 - Use AI to accelerate the testing process and test authoring, with disciplined human verification of coverage and correctness.

 - Take on light coding - such as fixing bugs and making small changes, with the help of an AI assistant.

 - Help your team in delivering a high quality product.

 - Participate and share your ideas in technical discussions.

 - Help your team make progress against its KPIs and the team’s North Star metric.

 - Develop your craft, build and share your experience in the field.



What You Bring

Before you read this next section, we want you to know that we know the “ideal candidate” doesn’t exist — everybody has a unique background and skill set. Even if you don’t check every box below, we’d love for you to apply - we are building for the future and growth potential is a priority for us!

 - Post-secondary education in the following areas preferred: Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Software Engineering or equivalent training/experience.

 - Good understanding of JavaScript/Typescript, and Python languages.

 - Good understanding of relational databases, especially SQL Server.

 - 3+ years of working experience in developing Integration Testing (API level) with Python or other languages.

 - 3+ years of working experience with Playwright or Selenium based tools.

 - Works AI-first with disciplined verification - uses AI to accelerate test design and authoring, and neither avoids it on principle nor trusts its output blindly.

 - Fluent with Git source control, and GitHub.

 - Passionate for building engaging user experiences.

 - Must be self-motivated, goal driven and possess the ability to multitask and prioritize.

 - A team player with a positive attitude and a willingness to contribute to the overall team’s success in a fast paced environment.

 - Experience with the following is a nice to have: exposure to Gatling, Postman, .NET Web stack: MVC, Web API, React, Redux, MongoDB, ElasticSearch and Redis, good understanding of OOD/OOP, and experience with Agile methodology, such as Kanban or Scrum.



Why You’ll Love Method

🌐 Flexible hybrid work model – designed to support work-life balance while maintaining strong team collaboration; employees work from our downtown Toronto office 2–3 days per week

🎉 Collaborative and social team culture – work in a supportive environment with

notes: Method CRM is a product-heavy organization with a strong focus on AI-powered tools and no-code app development. The job postings suggest a growing Development team and a need for skilled engineers and product managers.
